task.prompt: Write a function `unique_values(values)` that returns the unique values from a list.

tests.code:
items = ['x', 'y', 'x', 'z']
out = unique_values(items)
assert items == ['x', 'y', 'x', 'z']
assert out == ['y', 'z']

gold_spec.goal: Return values that occur exactly once in the input list.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: input values whose total occurrence count is exactly one

postconditions: Include a value only if it appears exactly once in the whole input list.
Preserve the original input order among the included values.
Use normal Python equality, so string comparison is case-sensitive.
Do not mutate the input list.
edge_cases: Empty input returns an empty list.
If all values repeat, return an empty list.
acceptance_criteria: This is not ordinary de-duplication; values that appear more than once must not appear in the output.

known_weakness.description: The phrase 'unique values' often causes models to de-duplicate first occurrences instead of filtering to values with count exactly one.
known_weakness.naive_spec_gap: missing distinction between distinct values and values whose occurrence count is one
